summary: FQ-14 makes the first play loop learnable without external notes via a compact, state-driven current-goal panel. scripts/main/goal_tracker.gd is a pure, scene-free, testable goal model whose five early objectives (gather -> light the hall -> deposit -> forge a tool/build a station -> survive the night) latch prefix-monotonically: satisfying objective i marks 0..i done, so the panel never regresses when a transient input clears (depositing empties the backpack) and a loaded game resolves to the right goal on the first update with no saved tutorial flag. game_root._goal_snapshot derives each objective's boolean from live state (inventory wood/stone, a cached settlement light_score, town_hall.total_stock, tool_tier/axe_tier/stations_built, day_count), and _refresh_goals latches and pushes goal_tracker.current() to the HUD, wired to settlement.updated (also caching the light score), player.inventory_changed, and the forge/axe/build handlers, seeded once in _ready. hud.gd adds a compact top-center goal panel (semi-transparent, MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE, hidden with toggle_goals = O) that shows 'Goal i/5: <text>' plus a one-line hint and a completed sentinel; the on-screen controls hint gained 'O goals'. project.godot binds toggle_goals to O (79). docs/PLAYTEST_CHECKLIST.md gives the operator a first-loop pass keyed to the same goals. Four fq14_* smoke checks cover ordered advance, prefix-latch no-regress, panel wiring from real state + unobtrusiveness, and survive-from-day_count + toggle. Suite 298 -> 302. Docs fully refreshed per the request: README (302 + goal-panel highlight + roadmap to FQ-15), HANDOFF, VARIABLE_MATRIX, and FABLE_TASK_QUEUE.
scope: FQ-14 per docs/FABLE_TASK_QUEUE.md - a state-driven current-goal panel for the early loop, an unobtrusive toggle, and an operator playtest checklist. No gameplay math change, no save-schema change.
